URlted Acilon TO Throw all Spokane FREIGHT
TO 0. r. 4 n. IN order 10 Force Terms
From The Hill Railroads.

pages.raw_text:
The boycott placed upon the Great
Northern and Northern Pacific roads
by the Spokane Shippers association
at its meeting Saturday afternoon is
the one subject of discussion among
business men today


"Spokanes freight depot receipts
during the past year." said J. A.
Schiller. president of the shippe RS
association, this morning, amounted
to between 100000 and $4,000. 600.
and we think we can divert the major
portion of this business to the o. r.
Il N"


What It Means.


That is the meaning of the ship
pers' association, which was embodied
in the following resolutions signed
by 24-of the most prominent business
firms in Spokane:


Whereas, we know that rates of
freight to Spokane from eastern
points should never justly exceed pre-
vailing rates to terminal points. plus
25 per cent of the corresponding pre-
vailing rate from terminal points
back to Spokane; therefore. be it


Resolved That until such an ad-
justment of freight rates can be eff
fected without increasing any rates
now in effect TO Spokane from eastern
points We agree to give all our BUSH
ness to the 0. r. & N"


Result Of Action at Chicago.


This resolution was adopted" said
F. L. Goodall of the Whitehouse COM
pany, "as spontaneous outburst of
Indignation over the action of the
Chicago traffic managers meeting in
ignoring Spokane's requests. 1t was
not prearranged. We did not attend
the meeting with that purpose in
view. But the step has been taken
and I think there will be no backward
move until the railroads yield. All
present signed the resolutions. which
were offered by Fred H. Mason of
Holley, Mason, Marks e Co. The
rate suggested in our resolution was
